intro: Cirrhopetalum rubroguttatum, also known as the red-spotted Cirrhopetalum, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cirrhopetalum rubroguttatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to alleviate respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
